prompt: Write an Adams CMD script to build a four-bar linkage mechanism. It has three moving rigid links: a crank (100 mm long, 0.5 kg), a coupler (250 mm long, 0.5 kg), and a rocker (200 mm long, 0.5 kg). The crank is pinned to the ground at the origin; the rocker is pinned to the ground 300 mm to the right. Connect the free ends of the crank and rocker with the coupler via revolute joints. Drive the crank at a constant 360 degrees per second. Simulate for 2 seconds at 0.001 s steps.

assertions.text: Script creates a model with model create command
Three distinct rigid body parts are created (crank, coupler, rocker)
Four revolute joints are created (crank-ground, crank-coupler, coupler-rocker, rocker-ground)
A motion generator drives the crank joint at a constant 360 degrees per second
Script includes simulate transient command (or notes the command to run)
All keywords spelled out fully — no abbreviated commands anywhere
All object names use full dot-path hierarchy (.model.part.marker)
No adams_id specified anywhere in the script
Mass/inertia set with part modify rigid_body mass_properties, not part create
center_of_mass_marker argument is NOT passed in any part modify mass_properties call
